title: High Rise Shelf
summary: A small wall shelf where the shelves float off a back board on spacer blocks — a West Elm-style display piece from two short boards and an afternoon.
description:
This is a quick, high-impact wall shelf: a 1x4 back board with short spacer blocks that hold a row of little 1x8 shelves proud of the wall, so they appear to float in a stepped, gallery-ish arrangement. It knocks off a pricey retailer look for the cost of two six-foot boards and a handful of screws.

The only technique worth naming is hiding your fasteners. Screw the spacers to the back board from behind, through the back, so no screw heads show on the front — predrill so the short blocks don't split. If you'd rather, a brad nailer works here too. The shelves themselves can be centered on their spacers or offset for a more dynamic look; there's no wrong answer, so lay them out before committing.

Hanging is the one place to be careful because a shelf carries whatever you set on it. Screw through the front of the shelf into a wall stud where you can; where there's no stud, use a proper drywall anchor rated for the load. Done right it's genuinely sturdy despite looking delicate — a nice display piece for small plants, books, or objects.

tools.note: Predrill the short spacer blocks so they don't split.
So the shelf hangs true on the wall.

materials.name: 1x8 board, 6 ft
materials.note: Shelves. A 1x6 or 1x12 works too — adjust shelf length.

materials.name: Drywall anchors
materials.note: Where you can't hit a stud.

cutList.note: A 1x6 or 1x12 lets you go shallower or deeper.

steps.title: Attach the spacers
steps.body: Lay out the spacer positions on the back board and mark them. Glue the less-attractive face of each spacer to the back board and screw from behind through the back board so no heads show on the front — predrill the short blocks so they don't split. A brad nailer works here too.

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Set each little shelf on its spacers, centered or offset as you like — lay them all out and eyeball the arrangement first — then screw them in place.

steps.title: Finish and hang
steps.body: Sand and finish the shelf. To hang, screw through the front of a shelf into a wall stud where you can; where there's no stud, use a drywall anchor rated for the load. Level it, and it'll be far sturdier than it looks.

images.alt: Small wall shelf with shelves floating off a back board on spacers
